output.summary: Accessibility review and test page update plan for QuickLinks block. Identified 5 accessibility gaps in the current implementation: missing <nav> landmark for the links column, missing list semantics (<ul>/<li>) for link items, missing screen reader indication for external links, focus:outline-none without visible replacement in PHP, and inconsistent hover/focus class handling between TSX and PHP. Test page needs comprehensive restructuring with DemoContainer wrapping and additional test scenarios.

requirementsChecked: Semantic HTML elements (landmarks pattern: nav for navigation links)
List semantics for groups of links (accessibility pattern: use <ul>/<ol> instead of <div> list)
Link pattern: native <a> with href (correctly implemented)
Keyboard support: Enter to activate links (native <a> behavior, correct)
Focus indicators visible on all interactive elements
Icon accessibility: aria-hidden on decorative icons
External link indication for screen readers
Section landmark: aria-labelledby on <section> (auto-applied by theme_block_props)
accessibilityReview.currentImplementationStatus: Partially compliant. Core link semantics are correct (native <a> elements with href). Section-level aria-labelledby is auto-applied by theme_block_props. However, missing <nav> landmark, missing list semantics, and inconsistent focus styling need attention.

gapsIdentified.description: Links column missing <nav> landmark. The right column containing navigational links is rendered as a plain <div>. Per the WAI-ARIA landmarks pattern, a collection of navigational links should be wrapped in a <nav> element to be discoverable by assistive technology users navigating by landmarks.

Link items missing list semantics. Individual link cards are rendered as <a> elements directly inside a <div>. Per accessibility best practices, a group of links should use <ul>/<li> structure so screen readers announce 'list, N items' and users can navigate by list shortcuts. The <li> wrapping around each <a> improves discoverability.

Missing <nav> aria-label. When the <nav> landmark is added, it should include an aria-label (e.g., 'Quick links') to distinguish it from other navigation landmarks on the page (like site nav). The heading attribute can serve as the source for this label.

PHP template has focus:outline-none on quick-link-item without a visible custom focus indicator. While focus:bg-accent and focus:border-accent provide some visual indication, the outline-none removes the browser's default focus ring. Should use focus-visible:outline or keep the browser default alongside the color changes. The TSX version does NOT have focus:outline-none, creating a sync mismatch.

External links (opensInNewTab=true) lack screen reader indication. When a link opens in a new tab, screen reader users should be informed via sr-only text like '(opens in new tab)' appended to the link content. Currently only target=_blank and rel=noopener are set.

recommendedFixes.description: Wrap the links column in a <nav> element instead of <div>. In PHP, change the outer <div> to <nav>. In TSX, change the wrapper div to <nav>.

Change the inner blocks wrapper from <div> to <ul> in both QuickLinks.tsx and QuickLinks.php. Change QuickLinkItem to render as <li> containing <a> instead of just <a>. The <a> tag becomes a child of <li>.
Add aria-label to the <nav> element. Use 'Quick links' as default label, or derive from the heading attribute if available.

Remove focus:outline-none from QuickLinkItem.php. The browser default focus ring plus the accent background/border changes provide adequate focus indication. This also syncs PHP with TSX which does not have focus:outline-none.
Add sr-only span with '(opens in new tab)' text inside the link when opensInNewTab is true in QuickLinkItem.php.

fileChanges.description: Wrap links column in <nav> with aria-label, change inner wrapper to <ul>
fileChanges.details: 1. Change the links column outer <div> to <nav aria-label="Quick links"> (or derive from heading if available). 2. Change the inner <div class="flex flex-col gap-4 self-start"> to <nav aria-label="..." class="flex flex-col gap-4 self-start">. 3. Ensure $children renders inside a <ul class="flex flex-col gap-4"> wrapper. NOTE: Since $children is a rendered string from inner blocks, the <ul> wrapper goes around $children echo, and each QuickLinkItem.php must output <li> wrapping.

fileChanges.description: Wrap links column in <nav> with aria-label, change inner blocks wrapper to <ul>
fileChanges.details: 1. Change the outer wrapper <div> for links column to <nav>. 2. Add aria-label prop derived from heading attribute or a static 'Quick links' string. 3. Change useInnerBlocksProps container from <div> to <ul> with className 'flex flex-col gap-4'. Must keep TSX/PHP sync.

fileChanges.description: Wrap <a> in <li>, remove focus:outline-none, add sr-only new tab text
fileChanges.details:
1. Wrap the entire element in
  • . Move the quick-link-item class and styling to the only (keep the card styling on ). 2. Remove focus:outline-none from the class string. 3. Add (opens in new tab) inside the when $opensInNewTab is true.

fileChanges.description: Wrap the block in <li> element, sync with PHP changes
fileChanges.details: 1. Change useBlockProps to use 'li' as the tag or wrap the content in <li>. NOTE: useBlockProps returns props for a div by default. The approach should be: keep useBlockProps on a <li> element and place the <a>-styled card inside it. Alternatively, since this is an inner block, the useBlockProps wrapper can be a <li>. 2. Ensure class sync with PHP.

currentScenarios: Single variation with full content (eyebrow, heading, description, button, 4 link items)
missingScenarios: Empty optional fields (no eyebrow, no description, no buttons)
Long content edge case (100+ char heading, long link titles)

interactiveTests.name: Link hover state shows accent colors
interactiveTests.expect: First link card shows accent background color and accent border color on hover

interactiveTests.name: Link focus state matches hover state
interactiveTests.expect: First link card shows accent background and border on focus, matching hover appearance

interactiveTests.name: Keyboard tab navigation through links
interactiveTests.expect: Focus moves to second link item, with visible focus indicator

designDecisions.decision: Add <nav> landmark wrapping the links column
designDecisions.evidence: Landmarks pattern specifies <nav> for 'Collection of navigational links'. The quick links column is a clear navigation pattern.

designDecisions.decision: Add <ul>/<li> list semantics for link items
designDecisions.evidence: Accessibility pattern states: 'Use <ul>, <ol> instead of <div> list - Announces count, navigation'. Link items are a list of navigational options.

designDecisions.decision: Use heading text as nav aria-label, falling back to 'Quick links'
designDecisions.evidence: Nav elements should have an aria-label to distinguish from other nav landmarks. The heading provides contextual naming.

designDecisions.decision: Remove focus:outline-none from PHP, keep browser default focus ring alongside accent color changes
designDecisions.evidence: Accessibility pattern states: 'Visible focus indicator on all interactive elements' and 'never remove outline without replacement'. PHP has focus:outline-none but no custom outline replacement; TSX does not have it. Removing it syncs PHP/TSX and preserves focus visibility.

designDecisions.decision: Add sr-only '(opens in new tab)' text for external links
designDecisions.evidence: Screen reader users need to know when a link opens in a new window/tab. This is a WCAG best practice (Success Criterion 3.2.5).

designDecisions.decision: QuickLinkItem uses <li> as the block wrapper with the <a> card inside
designDecisions.evidence: To enable list semantics while keeping the card-style link, the <li> serves as the list item container and the <a> provides the full-card link behavior. This is a standard pattern for accessible link lists.
